body.answer: An "exclusive" lead can be old, dead, or sold to a dozen pros — relabeling is the whole trick.
body.html:
A lead seller buys a giant list of homeowners who once clicked a form. Some of those clicks are weeks or months old. Some people already hired someone. Some never wanted the work at all. The seller slaps the word exclusive and fresh on top and charges you premium money for it.

Here is the tell: a truly exclusive lead goes to one pro. Shared platforms tell you up front how many pros get the same lead. Thumbtack sends a lead to 4-5 pros and Angi sends to 2-4 pros [2026]. Google Local Services Ads (LSA) are exclusive [2026]. When a no-name seller claims "exclusive" but won't say how the lead was sourced or how old it is, you are likely buying recycled goods at an exclusive price.

body.h2: Why aged leads cost you more than they look
body.answer: Speed is everything, and an old lead has already gone cold before it hits your phone.
body.html:
The whole value of a lead is being first and being fast. 78% of homeowners hire the FIRST contractor to respond [Lead Connect 2026]. Responding in 5 minutes makes a lead 100x more likely to qualify than responding at 30 minutes [MIT Sloan 2026]. An aged lead already blew past both of those windows days ago. You are calling a corpse.

It also poisons your close rate. Shared leads convert at just 6-10%, while organic leads convert at 18-24% and a direct call up to 40% [2026]. A recycled "exclusive" lead behaves like the worst end of shared — you pay top dollar and close like bottom dollar.

body.h2: Know the real cost per booked job
body.answer: Compare lead sources by cost per BOOKED job, not cost per lead, or you'll get fooled.
body.html:
A cheap lead that never books is the most expensive lead there is. Look at what it actually costs to land one paying job. Google LSA comes in best at about $168 per booked job because it's exclusive [SearchLight Digital 2026]. Thumbtack runs about $250 per booked job, shared across 4-5 pros [2026]. Angi/HomeAdvisor — the same company — runs about $542 per booked job, with customer acquisition cost climbing as high as $2,500 [2026].

Owned SEO sits around $290-310 per booked job [2026]. Notice that the random "exclusive" lead seller isn't on this list — because nobody can verify their real cost per booked job. If they could prove it, they'd lead with it.

body.h2: How to test a lead source before you pay
body.answer: Buy a tiny batch first, time-stamp every lead, and measure book rate before you commit a dollar more.
body.html:
Treat any new lead source like a job site you've never been to: inspect before you commit. Run a small test buy. Then check four things:

1. Age. Ask exactly when the homeowner submitted. If the seller dodges the date, the lead is old. 2. Exclusivity. Make them put in writing how many pros get it. Remember LSA is exclusive and Thumbtack is 4-5 pros [2026] — if a seller claims "exclusive" cheaper than LSA, be suspicious. 3. Contact rate. Call every test lead within 5 minutes and track how many are real, reachable people. 4. Book rate. Divide jobs booked by leads bought. If it lands near the shared-lead floor of 6-10% [2026], you're buying recycled leads.

One more guardrail: legitimate networks issue refunds for bad leads. Angi/HomeAdvisor refunds 15-22% of leads as credits [2026]. A seller with no refund policy is telling you they already know the leads are junk.

body.h2: Build the asset that can't be resold
body.answer: Your own Google presence and reviews are leads nobody else can recycle and sell back to you.
body.html:
The reason aged-lead scams work is that you don't have your own flow of fresh demand, so you rent it. Fix the rent problem. Owned SEO costs about $290-310 per booked job [2026] and the lead is yours alone — it can't be sold to five pros.

Reviews are the other moat. 91% of people read local reviews and most won't consider a business under 4 stars [BrightLocal 2025], and 81% rely on Google reviews [CallRail 2026]. To even show up on page 1 of Google Maps you need volume: the median page-1 contractor has 519 Google reviews for HVAC, 337 for plumbing, and 144 for roofing [Booked Job GBP scrape, 2026, major TX metros]. That is get found, get picked, get booked — and no lead broker can take it from you. More at booked-job.com.

faq.q: How can I tell if an "exclusive" lead is actually shared or recycled?
faq.a: Make the seller state in writing how many pros receive it and the exact date the homeowner submitted. For reference, LSA leads are exclusive and Thumbtack sends to 4-5 pros [2026]. If a seller claims "exclusive" but priced below LSA's roughly $168 per booked job [SearchLight Digital 2026], or won't share the submission date, treat it as recycled.

faq.q: What's a fair way to test a new lead source?
faq.a: Buy a small batch, call every lead within 5 minutes, and track your book rate. Responding in 5 minutes makes a lead 100x more likely to qualify than 30 minutes [MIT Sloan 2026]. If your book rate sits near the shared-lead floor of 6-10% [2026], the source isn't worth premium money.

faq.q: Why is owning my own lead flow better than buying leads?
faq.a: Because nobody can resell it back to you. Owned SEO runs about $290-310 per booked job and organic leads convert at 18-24% versus 6-10% for shared [2026]. Paired with reviews — 81% of buyers rely on Google reviews [CallRail 2026] — your own presence is a lead source no broker controls.

title: Aged Leads Resold as "Exclusive": The Marketing Scam and How to Test a Source | Booked Job
h1: The exclusive lead scam: how old, dead, and recycled leads get resold as fresh
meta: Aged leads get relabeled "exclusive" and sold at a premium. Learn the tells and how to test any lead source by cost per booked job before you pay.
short: Some sellers take old, dead, or recycled leads, call them "exclusive" and "fresh," and charge premium money. A real exclusive lead goes to one pro. Test any source first: buy a small batch, demand the submission date, and measure your book rate before you spend more.
statLabel: cost per booked job on Angi/HomeAdvisor vs ~$168 on exclusive Google LSA [SearchLight Digital 2026]

_verdict.notes: Every numeric and dollar claim in the article maps to the approved list with correct source attributions. Verified: $542 Angi/HomeAdvisor + $168 LSA [SearchLight Digital 2026] in statBig; CAC up to $2,500 and refunds 15-22% [2026]; Thumbtack ~$250/4-5 pros, Angi 2-4 pros, LSA exclusive [2026]; owned SEO $290-310 [2026]; 78% first-to-respond [Lead Connect 2026]; 5-min=100x vs 30 min [MIT Sloan 2026]; shared 6-10%/organic 18-24%/direct up to 40% [2026]; 91% read reviews + under-4-stars [BrightLocal 2025]; 81% Google reviews [CallRail 2026]; GBP page-1 medians HVAC 519/plumbing 337/roofing 144 [Booked Job GBP scrape 2026, major TX metros]. No fabricated stats, no invented illustrative dollar amounts, no wrong attributions. The LSA-to-SearchLight-Digital attribution is correctly applied per the approved bracket note. Tone is aggressive ("calling a corpse," "scam," "recycled goods") but on-brand for the combative service-pro audience and appropriate for a scam-exposé topic; no harmful or misleading tone.
